question: Across January of 3 years from the derived year (2019) to the year from 2017-2022 that has the highest total daily census records from Allegheny County Jail to the year from 2017-2022 that has the lowest total ridership reported by the Rochester Genesee Regional Transportation Authority, identify the issuing agencies that consistently ranked in the top 20 by total parking-violation fines. From those, keep only the U.S. national law-enforcement bodies and find which had the highest average number of January moving violations. That agency is operated by a federal agency within a U.S. department. What is the department's name?
answer: United States Department of the Interior

1.fact: Counting records by year from census_date for 2017-2022, the maximum total is 1202002 in 2019.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2017-2022 has the highest total daily census records from Allegheny County Jail? (Use census_date to derive year; count records by year and select the maximum.)

2.fact: Summing Ridership across all subsidiaries by Year for 2017-2022, the minimum total is 7778388 in 2021.
2.subquestion: Which year from 2017-2022 has the lowest total ridership reported by the Rochester Genesee Regional Transportation Authority? (Sum Ridership by Year across all subsidiaries and select the minimum.)

3.fact: According to the January 2019 DC parking violations dataset, the top 20 issuing agencies by total fine amount were: DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS ($4,954,065.00), METROPOLITAN POLICE DPT-DISTRICT 2 ($77,631.00), METROPOLITAN POLICE DPT-DISTRICT 1 ($63,915.00), METROPOLITAN POLICE DPT-DISTRICT 4 ($59,570.00), METROPOLITAN POLICE DPT-DISTRICT 5 ($47,620.00), METROPOLITAN POLICE DPT-DISTRICT 7 ($47,050.00), D.C. HOUSING AUTHORITY ($45,080.00), METROPOLITAN POLICE DPT-DISTRICT 3 ($44,695.00), METROPOLITAN POLICE DPT-DISTRICT 6 ($28,595.00), SPECIAL OPERATION DIV & TRAFFIC DIV ($20,030.00), UNITED STATES CAPITOL POLICE ($15,230.00), METRO POLICE ($12,690.00), DDOT ($12,095.00), PROTECTIVE SERVICES DEPT (DC GOVT) ($9,760.00), SPECIAL INSPECTION DIVISION ($5,550.00), MPD RESERVE CORPS ($4,620.00), UNVRSTY OF THE D.C. SECURITY POLICE ($3,510.00), TAXI COMMISSION ($2,700.00), US. SECRET SERVICE UNIFORM DIVISION ($2,695.00), and UNITED STATES PARK POLICE ($2,245.00).
3.subquestion: Which agencies were in the top 20 for total January parking-violation fine amount in the year from node 1 (2019) to the year from node 2 (2021)? (Filter for this node: group by ISSUING_AGENCY_NAME; sum FINE_AMOUNT; rank desc; take top 20.)
answer: UNVRSTY OF THE D.C. SECURITY POLICE

9.fact: The United States Park Police is an operation of the National Park Service.
9.subquestion: Which federal agency operates the United States Park Police (from hop 2)?

10.fact: The National Park Service is an agency within the United States Department of the Interior.
10.subquestion: The agency (from hop 3) is within which U.S. department?
10.answer: United States Department of the Interior

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Year with highest total daily jail census records (2017-2022) -> 2019
Node 2: Year with lowest total ridership (2017-2022) -> 2021
Node 3: 2019 top 20 issuing agencies by total fine
Node 4: 2020 top 20 issuing agencies by total fine
Node 5: 2021 top 20 issuing agencies by total fine
Intersection: agencies consistently top 20; keep U.S. national law-enforcement bodies -> {United States Park Police, United States Capitol Police, U.S. Secret Service Uniform Division}
Node 6: 2019 moving violations for the three agencies
Node 7: 2020 moving violations for the three agencies
Node 8: 2021 moving violations and average across 2019-2021 -> United States Park Police highest
Node 9: United States Park Police is operated by the National Park Service
Node 10: National Park Service is within the United States Department of the Interior
Final answer: United States Department of the Interior
